input.command: ls cli/internal/agent/ | wc -l; ls cli/internal/agent/ | grep -E "(types|options|runtime)"

data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
11	type Config struct {
12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
21	}
22	
23	// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
25	type RouteFlags struct {
26		Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
28		Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
29		ModelRef    string // --model-ref: catalog model reference
30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
33	}
34	
35	// RunOptions holds options for a single agent invocation.
36	type RunOptions struct {
37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context
42		Harness       string
43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
45		PromptSource  string
46		Correlation   map[string]string
47		Model         string
48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
49		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved via the model catalog
50		Effort        string
51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
53		WorkDir       string
54		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
55		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
56	}
57	
58	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunOptions for the agent
59	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
60	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
61	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
62	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
63	// runtime intent remain.
64	//
65	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
66	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
67		Prompt                string
68		PromptFile            string
69		PromptSource          string
70		Output                io.Writer
71		Correlation           map[string]string
72		Record                bool
73		Replay                bool
74		WorkDir               string
75		EstimatedPromptTokens int
76		RequiresTools         bool
77		SessionLogDirOverride string
78	}
79	
80	// QuorumOptions extends RunOptions for multi-agent consensus.
81	type QuorumOptions struct {
82		RunOptions
83		Harnesses []string // multiple harnesses to invoke
84		Strategy  string   // any, majority, unanimous, or numeric
85		Threshold int      // numeric threshold (when Strategy is "")
86	}
87	
88	// Result holds the output of an agent invocation.
89	type Result struct {
90		Harness         string `json:"harness"`
91		Provider        string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
92		Model           string `json:"model,omitempty"`
93		ExitCode        int    `json:"exit_code"`
94		Output          string `json:"output"`
95		CondensedOutput string `json:"condensed_output,omitempty"`
96		Stderr          string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
97		// Routing evidence populated by the embedded agent harness (RunAgent) or
98		// the script harness (RunScript). Used by ExecuteBead to record kind:routing
99		// evidence on the bead.
100		RouteReason     string          `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
101		ResolvedBaseURL string          `json:"resolved_base_url,omitempty"`
102		Tokens          int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
103		InputTokens     int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
104		OutputTokens    int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
105		CostUSD         float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
106		DurationMS      int             `json:"duration_ms"`
107		Error           string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
108		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`       // populated by agent, nil for subprocess
109		AgentSessionID  string          `json:"agent_session_id,omitempty"` // agent session ID for event log cross-reference
110	}
111	
112	// SessionEntry is written to the session log.
113	type SessionEntry struct {
114		ID              string            `json:"id"`
115		Timestamp       time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
116		Harness         string            `json:"harness"`
117		Provider        string            `json:"provider,omitempty"`
118		Surface         string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`
119		CanonicalTarget string            `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
120		BaseURL         string            `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
121		BillingMode     string            `json:"billingMode,omitempty"`
122		Model           string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
123		PromptLen       int               `json:"prompt_len"`
124		Prompt          string            `json:"prompt,omitempty"`
125		PromptSource    string            `json:"prompt_source,omitempty"`
126		Response        string            `json:"response,omitempty"`
127		Correlation     map[string]string `json:"correlation,omitempty"`
128		NativeSessionID string            `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
129		NativeLogRef    string            `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`
130		TraceID         string            `json:"trace_id,omitempty"`
131		SpanID          string            `json:"span_id,omitempty"`
132		Stderr          string            `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
133		Tokens          int               `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
134		InputTokens     int               `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
135		OutputTokens    int               `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
136		CostUSD         float64           `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
137		Duration        int               `json:"duration_ms"`
138		ExitCode        int               `json:"exit_code"`
139		Error           string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
140		TotalTokens     int               `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"` // input + output; populated on every run
141		BaseRev         string            `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`     // git SHA the execution started from (execute-bead only)
142		ResultRev       string            `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`   // git SHA of landed/preserved iteration (execute-bead only)
143	}
144	
145	// ProviderStatus tracks provider connectivity and credit status.
146	type ProviderStatus struct {
147		Reachable bool   `json:"reachable"`
148		CreditsOK bool   `json:"credits_ok,omitempty"` // false if out of credits/quota
149		Error     string `json:"error,omitempty"`
150	}
151	
152	// HarnessCapabilities describes the effective capabilities for a harness.
153	type HarnessCapabilities struct {
154		Harness             string            `json:"harness"`
155		Available           bool              `json:"available"`
156		Binary              string            `json:"binary"`
157		Path                string            `json:"path,omitempty"`
158		Model               string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
159		Models              []string          `json:"models,omitempty"`
160		ReasoningLevels     []string          `json:"reasoning_levels,omitempty"`
161		Surface             string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`          // catalog surface identifier
162		CostClass           string            `json:"cost_class,omitempty"`       // local, cheap, medium, expensive
163		IsLocal             bool              `json:"is_local"`                   // true if embedded/local (no cloud cost)
164		ExactPinSupport     bool              `json:"exact_pin_support"`          // true if harness accepts exact model pin
165		ProfileMappings     map[string]string `json:"profile_mappings,omitempty"` // effective profile → model for this harness
166		SupportsEffort      bool              `json:"supports_effort"`            // true if harness has effort/reasoning flag
167		SupportsPermissions bool              `json:"supports_permissions"`       // true if harness has permission-level flags
168	}
169	
170	// CompareOptions configures a comparison dispatch.
171	type CompareOptions struct {
172		RunOptions
173		Harnesses   []string       // harnesses to compare (may include duplicates with different models)
174		ArmModels   map[int]string // per-arm model overrides keyed by arm index
175		ArmLabels   map[int]string // per-arm display labels (e.g. "claude-fast")
176		Sandbox     bool           // run each arm in an isolated worktree
177		KeepSandbox bool           // preserve worktrees after comparison
178		PostRun     string         // command to run in each worktree after the agent completes
179	}
180	
181	// ToolCallEntry records one tool execution during an agent run.
182	// Mirrors the agent library's ToolCallLog without importing it in types.
183	type ToolCallEntry struct {
184		Tool     string `json:"tool"`
185		Input    string `json:"input"`
186		Output   string `json:"output,omitempty"`
187		Duration int    `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
188		Error    string `json:"error,omitempty"`
189	}
190	
191	// ComparisonArm holds the result of one harness arm in a comparison.
192	type ComparisonArm struct {
193		Harness      string          `json:"harness"`
194		Model        string          `json:"model,omitempty"`
195		Output       string          `json:"output"`
196		Diff         string          `json:"diff,omitempty"`         // git diff of side effects
197		ToolCalls    []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`   // agent tool call log (nil for subprocess)
198		PostRunOut   string          `json:"post_run_out,omitempty"` // post-run command output
199		PostRunOK    *bool           `json:"post_run_ok,omitempty"`  // post-run pass/fail
200		Tokens       int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
201		InputTokens  int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
202		OutputTokens int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
203		CostUSD      float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
204		DurationMS   int             `json:"duration_ms"`
205		ExitCode     int             `json:"exit_code"`
206		Error        string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
207	}
208	
209	// ComparisonGrade holds the evaluation of one arm by a grading harness.
210	type ComparisonGrade struct {
211		Arm       string `json:"arm"`
212		Score     int    `json:"score"`
213		MaxScore  int    `json:"max_score"`
214		Pass      bool   `json:"pass"`
215		Rationale string `json:"rationale"`
216	}
217	
218	// ComparisonRecord is the complete record of a comparison run.
219	type ComparisonRecord struct {
220		ID        string            `json:"id"`
221		Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
222		Prompt    string            `json:"prompt"`
223		Arms      []ComparisonArm   `json:"arms"`
224		Grades    []ComparisonGrade `json:"grades,omitempty"`
225	}
226	
227	// CandidateState captures contract-derived routing state for a candidate.
228	type CandidateState struct {
229		Installed     bool                   `json:"installed"`
230		Reachable     bool                   `json:"reachable"`
231		Authenticated bool                   `json:"authenticated"`
232		QuotaOK       bool                   `json:"quota_ok"`
233		QuotaState    string                 `json:"quota_state,omitempty"` // ok, blocked, unknown
234		Degraded      bool                   `json:"degraded"`
235		PolicyOK      bool                   `json:"policy_ok"`
236		LastChecked   time.Time              `json:"last_checked,omitempty"`
237		Error         string                 `json:"error,omitempty"`
238		Quota         *QuotaInfo             `json:"quota,omitempty"`
239		RoutingSignal *RoutingSignalSnapshot `json:"routing_signal,omitempty"`
240	}
241	
242	// QuotaInfo holds parsed quota data from CLI introspection.
243	type QuotaInfo struct {
244		PercentUsed int    `json:"percent_used"`
245		LimitWindow string `json:"limit_window,omitempty"` // e.g. "5h", "7 day"
246		ResetDate   string `json:"reset_date,omitempty"`   // e.g. "April 12"
247	}
248	
249	// AccountInfo captures provider account metadata from local auth files.
250	type AccountInfo struct {
251		Email    string `json:"email,omitempty"`
252		PlanType string `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
253		OrgName  string `json:"org_name,omitempty"`
254	}
255	
256	// QuotaWindow captures one quota window (e.g. 5h, weekly, model-specific).
257	type QuotaWindow struct {
258		Name          string  `json:"name"`               // e.g. "5h", "7d", "spark"
259		LimitID       string  `json:"limit_id,omitempty"` // provider limit_id
260		WindowMinutes int     `json:"window_minutes"`
261		UsedPercent   float64 `json:"used_percent"`
262		ResetsAt      string  `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`      // human-readable
263		ResetsAtUnix  int64   `json:"resets_at_unix,omitempty"` // unix timestamp
264		State         string  `json:"state"`
265	}
266	
267	// SignalSourceMetadata captures where a routing signal came from and how
268	// fresh it is. Populated from upstream HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo.
269	type SignalSourceMetadata struct {
270		Provider   string    `json:"provider"`
271		Kind       string    `json:"kind"`
272		Path       string    `json:"path,omitempty"`
273		ObservedAt time.Time `json:"observed_at,omitempty"`
274		Freshness  string    `json:"freshness"`
275		AgeSeconds int64     `json:"age_seconds,omitempty"`
276		Basis      string    `json:"basis,omitempty"`
277		Notes      string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
278	}
279	
280	// QuotaSignal captures current quota/headroom for a harness.
281	type QuotaSignal struct {
282		Source        SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
283		State         string               `json:"state"`
284		UsedPercent   int                  `json:"used_percent,omitempty"`
285		WindowMinutes int                  `json:"window_minutes,omitempty"`
286		ResetsAt      string               `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`
287	}
288	
289	// UsageSignal captures token/session totals for a harness.
290	type UsageSignal struct {
291		Source            SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
292		InputTokens       int                  `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
293		CachedInputTokens int                  `json:"cached_input_tokens,omitempty"`
294		OutputTokens      int                  `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
295		TotalTokens       int                  `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
296		SessionCount      int                  `json:"session_count,omitempty"`
297	}
298	
299	// RoutingSignalSnapshot captures routing signal metadata for a harness.
300	// Populated from upstream HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo data — no provider-native
301	// file parsing.
302	type RoutingSignalSnapshot struct {
303		Provider        string               `json:"provider"`
304		Source          SignalSourceMetadata `json:"source"`
305		CurrentQuota    QuotaSignal          `json:"current_quota,omitempty"`
306		HistoricalUsage UsageSignal          `json:"historical_usage,omitempty"`
307		Account         *AccountInfo         `json:"account,omitempty"`
308		QuotaWindows    []QuotaWindow        `json:"quota_windows,omitempty"`
309	}
310	
311	// RoutingOutcome is one bounded sample of DDx-observed routing performance.
312	type RoutingOutcome struct {
313		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
314		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
315		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
316		Model           string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
317		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
318		Success         bool      `json:"success"`
319		LatencyMS       int       `json:"latency_ms"`
320		InputTokens     int       `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
321		OutputTokens    int       `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
322		CostUSD         float64   `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
323		NativeSessionID string    `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
324		NativeLogRef    string    `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`
325		TraceID         string    `json:"trace_id,omitempty"`
326		SpanID          string    `json:"span_id,omitempty"`
327	}
328	
329	// QuotaSnapshot captures one quota/headroom sample for routing.
330	type QuotaSnapshot struct {
331		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
332		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
333		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
334		Source          string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
335		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
336		QuotaState      string    `json:"quota_state"`
337		UsedPercent     int       `json:"used_percent,omitempty"`
338		WindowMinutes   int       `json:"window_minutes,omitempty"`
339		ResetsAt        string    `json:"resets_at,omitempty"`
340		SampleKind      string    `json:"sample_kind"`
341	}
342	
343	// BurnSummary is a derived relative subscription-pressure estimate.
344	type BurnSummary struct {
345		Harness         string    `json:"harness"`
346		Surface         string    `json:"surface,omitempty"`
347		CanonicalTarget string    `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
348		ObservedAt      time.Time `json:"observed_at"`
349		BurnIndex       float64   `json:"burn_index"`
350		Trend           string    `json:"trend,omitempty"`
351		Confidence      float64   `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
352		Basis           string    `json:"basis,omitempty"`
353	}
354	
355	// RouteRequest is the normalized routing ask built from CLI flags and config.
356	type RouteRequest struct {
357		Profile         string // default, cheap, fast, smart
358		ModelRef        string // logical catalog ref or alias
359		ModelPin        string // exact concrete model string (bypasses catalog policy)
360		Effort          string // low, medium, high, etc.
361		Permissions     string // safe, supervised, unrestricted
362		HarnessOverride string // forces routing to one harness only
363	}
364	
365	// CandidatePlan is a routing evaluation result for one harness.
366	type CandidatePlan struct {
367		Harness               string         `json:"harness"`
368		Surface               string         `json:"surface,omitempty"`          // catalog surface: embedded-openai, embedded-anthropic, codex, claude
369		RequestedRef          string         `json:"requested_ref,omitempty"`    // profile or model ref from the request
370		CanonicalTarget       string         `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"` // resolved catalog canonical target
371		ConcreteModel         string         `json:"concrete_model,omitempty"`   // concrete model string to pass to harness
372		SupportsEffort        bool           `json:"supports_effort"`
373		SupportsPermissions   bool           `json:"supports_permissions"`
374		State                 CandidateState `json:"state"`
375		Provider              string         `json:"provider,omitempty"`            // discovered provider endpoint name (e.g. vidar, bragi)
376		CostClass             string         `json:"cost_class,omitempty"`          // local, cheap, medium, expensive
377		IsSubscription        bool           `json:"is_subscription,omitempty"`     // fixed-subscription harness; preferred over pay-per-token within quota
378		EstimatedCostUSD      float64        `json:"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty"`  // -1 = unknown
379		HistoricalSuccessRate float64        `json:"historical_success_rate"`       // observed success rate from routing metrics; -1 when insufficient data (< 3 samples)
380		RejectReason          string         `json:"reject_reason,omitempty"`       // non-empty means rejected
381		DeprecationWarning    string         `json:"deprecation_warning,omitempty"` // non-empty when requested ref is deprecated
382		Score                 float64        `json:"score,omitempty"`
383		Viable                bool           `json:"viable"`
384	}
385	
386	// Default configuration values.
387	const (
388		DefaultHarness = "codex"
389		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
390		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
391		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
392		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
393		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
394		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
395		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
396		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
397		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
398		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
399		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
400		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
401		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
402	)
403	
404	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
405	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
406	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
407	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
408	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
409		if configured == "" {
410			configured = DefaultLogDir
411		}
412		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
413			return configured
414		}
415		if projectRoot == "" {
416			return configured
417		}
418		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
419	}
420

input.command: grep -n "func BuildArgs\|func.*resolvePrompt\|func defaultResolvePromptForCompare\|func RunViaServiceWith\|func runVirtualFn\|func BenchmarkArmsToCompare\|func SessionIndexEntryFromResult" cli/internal/agent/*.go | head -20

data.text: Let me write a no_changes_rationale instead, because the AC is genuinely contradictory with the bead's stated test-only scope. First let me understand the full picture better.

input.content:
no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

data.text:
Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` rather than committing partial code.

**Summary:** The bead description scopes "test sites" (5–10) and the prior attempt (commit `5ae02964`) already migrated 26 sites across `agent_test.go`, `runner_test.go`, `script_test.go`. The reviewer's BLOCK reopened the bead because the AC's literal grep gate (`RunOptions\{` returns 0 matches globally) cannot be satisfied without:
- changing production-function signatures (`BuildArgs`, `resolvePrompt`, `defaultResolvePromptForCompare`, `RunViaServiceWith`, `runVirtualFn`, `BenchmarkArmsToCompare`, `SessionIndexEntryFromResult`) so the remaining test-side `RunOptions{}` constructions can disappear, **and**
- migrating ~14 production caller sites in `cmd/`, `internal/server/`, `internal/exec/`, `internal/agent/{runner,compare_adapter,grade,execute_bead,execute_bead_review}.go`.

That work is explicitly outside the bead's "test sites" scope and matches what the reviewer themselves recommended splitting into follow-up beads. The rationale file lays out (a) what's done, (b) the Class A / Class B blockers with file:line lists, and (c) the two follow-up options (narrow the AC vs. expand scope).
